relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Two officers of Pharaoh fell from favor into Joseph’s prison world.
Pharaoh’s anger came upon cupbearer and baker.
They were placed where Joseph was confined.
Joseph served them in custody.
Both men dreamed in one night, each dream carrying its own meaning.
Joseph noticed their troubled faces.
He asked why their faces were sad.
They had dreams without an interpreter, and Joseph pointed interpretation back to the Divine.
The cupbearer told of a vine before him.
Three branches blossomed and ripened into grapes.
The cup returned to Pharaoh’s hand through him.
Joseph read the three branches as three days.
The cupbearer would be lifted and restored.
Joseph asked to be remembered with covenant-kindness.
He named his own injustice: stolen from the Hebrews and placed in the pit without cause.
The baker, hearing good interpretation, offered his dream too.
Birds ate Pharaoh’s baked food from the basket above him.
Joseph read the three baskets as three days.
The baker’s lifting would not be restoration but judgment.
On the third day, Pharaoh lifted both heads before the servants.
The cupbearer was restored to Pharaoh’s cup.
The baker met the darker interpretation.
The cupbearer forgot Joseph.
